item.title: Pluralistic: Criticizing the everything machine (06 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow critiques the AI industry's tendency to make vast, unfounded claims that are impossible to address in short media segments. He argues that AI is a money-losing venture with worsening unit economics, sustained only by massive subsidies. Before discussing AI's future capabilities, we must consider whether anyone will fund them. He questions the environmental impact of data centers and the opportunity cost of diverting resources from proven research like cancer treatments. His upcoming book 'The Reverse Centaur's Guide to Life After AI' aims to provide a coherent critique of AI hype.
标签: AI, tech criticism, hype, economics, environment, labor, regulation, ethics, media, book

item.title: From Kepler to Bessel 摘要
item.description:
This article explains the connection between Kepler's equation and Bessel functions. Kepler's equation relates mean anomaly M and eccentric anomaly E in orbital mechanics, but has no elementary solution. One approach expands E in a sine series, where the Fourier coefficients turn out to be Bessel functions J_n(ne). The derivation uses integration by parts and the integral definition of Bessel functions, showing how solving orbital mechanics problems naturally leads to these special functions.
标签: mathematics, Bessel functions, Kepler's equation, orbital mechanics, Fourier series, numerical methods, astronomy, special functions

item.title: Trump Lawyer Argues Trump Can Tear Down Statue of Liberty 摘要
item.description:
In a court hearing, a judge asked if President Trump could bulldoze the Statue of Liberty without legal challenge. DOJ lawyer Yaakov Roth affirmed that Trump could do so, sparking criticism. Josh Marshall argues that the president is an employee of the people, not an owner, and cannot destroy national property. The article condemns the legal stance as absurd and sycophantic.
标签: politics, law, executive power, Trump, Statue of Liberty, DOJ, judicial review, property rights, constitutional law

item.title: 60 Minutes Correspondents Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker, and the Other Guy Will Stay at Show 摘要
item.description:
Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker, and Jon Wertheim wrote a memo to 60 Minutes staff explaining their decision to stay at the show despite being upset over the firings of Tanya and Draggan, whom they describe as strong leaders who fought for the program's values. They argue that newsrooms should not be run like dictatorships and that collaboration and argument are essential. They fear their return might be seen as endorsing the power structure, but they are staying to prevent the show from dying.
标签: 60 Minutes, journalism, media, CBS, newsroom culture, firings, integrity, independence, correspondents, memo

item.title: Update: ending paid subscriptions, + Substack 摘要
item.description:
Joan Westenberg announces she is turning off paid subscriptions to make her writing free and open to all. Her work is already funded by her agency, SELF, and her products. She plans to move her writing to Substack to leverage its network effects, podcasting, and video tools for growth. She thanks past supporters and invites readers to continue following her work.
标签: writing, subscriptions, Substack, free content, agency, monetization, blogging, creator economy, announcement
